issues.message: Avoid console statements in production code.
Avoid var — use const or let instead.
debugger statement found — remove before shipping.
Empty catch block silently swallows errors.
Function has too many parameters (6). Consider using an object or splitting the function.
issues.lineContent: function handleLogin(email, password, rememberMe, captcha, locale, timezone) {

issues.message: Low cohesion in "handleLogin": mixes HTTP, database, logging.
issues.lineContent: function handleLogin(email, password, rememberMe, captcha, locale, timezone) {

suggestions: Remove console statements or replace with a proper logging library.
Replace var with const (preferred) or let to use block-scoped declarations.
Remove all debugger statements before shipping to production.
Handle errors meaningfully or rethrow them.
Use an object parameter or split the function into smaller pieces.
Split into smaller, focused functions with a single responsibility.
summary: ❌ High risk: 2 critical issues detected. Not safe for production.
